categoryEnum.label: Runtime UX
categoryEnum.definition: Can a user launch, understand, and recover from the running game loop without fixture-specific knowledge?
categoryEnum.exampleFinding: A user cannot find how to start/restart/play the loop.

categoryEnum.label: Renderer Quality
categoryEnum.definition: Does the rendered output communicate game state clearly enough for practical play and review?
categoryEnum.exampleFinding: Sprites, camera, scaling, or visual ordering make gameplay unreadable.

categoryEnum.label: Input / Control
categoryEnum.definition: Do input mappings, responsiveness, replay, and control affordances support the claimed gameplay/editor action?
categoryEnum.exampleFinding: Required actions are missing, laggy, undiscoverable, or not replayable.

categoryEnum.label: Scene Authoring
categoryEnum.definition: Can scenes, levels, entities, components, and layout edits be authored and reviewed through the scoped product flow?
categoryEnum.exampleFinding: A level change requires hand-editing fixture JSON despite an editor-authoring claim.

categoryEnum.label: Asset Workflow
categoryEnum.definition: Can assets be referenced, previewed, validated, and diagnosed in the scoped product flow?
categoryEnum.exampleFinding: Missing/invalid assets fail silently or previews cannot be tied to source refs.

categoryEnum.label: Behavior / Scripting
categoryEnum.definition: Can gameplay behavior be expressed, constrained, verified, and debugged without unsafe arbitrary code expansion?
categoryEnum.exampleFinding: A behavior claim depends on opaque scripts or has no deterministic assertion path.

categoryEnum.label: Editor Workflow
categoryEnum.definition: Can Studio/editor users navigate projects, inspect state, draft changes, and understand gated writes?
categoryEnum.exampleFinding: The editor surface displays data but gives no usable workflow for the claimed task.

categoryEnum.label: Agentic Mutation
categoryEnum.definition: Can proposal, review, apply/rerun, rollback, and backlog steps be observed and bounded end to end?
categoryEnum.exampleFinding: An agent proposes changes without before/after evidence or human review gate proof.

categoryEnum.label: QA / Evaluator Depth
categoryEnum.definition: Do evaluators catch mechanical, runtime, visual, semantic, regression, and product-observed failures with honest gaps?
categoryEnum.exampleFinding: Green tests miss a visible product failure or collapse multiple failure modes into one vague verdict.

categoryEnum.label: Export / Package Reality
categoryEnum.definition: Does export/package evidence reflect a runnable local artifact without implying store submission or release authority?
categoryEnum.exampleFinding: A bundle descriptor exists but no local smoke proves it runs.

categoryEnum.label: Dogfood Game Quality
categoryEnum.definition: Does a real dogfood game demonstrate coherent play, pacing, feedback, and human-owned fun/release judgment?
categoryEnum.exampleFinding: A dogfood slice is mechanically passable but confusing, unfun, or lacks human verdict evidence.

severityEnum.definition: Prevents the scoped user/product flow from being completed or makes the claim materially false.
severityEnum.observableExample: Runtime cannot be launched from the documented entry point; editor cannot open the project; agentic apply lacks review gate evidence.

severityEnum.definition: The flow can complete only with significant workaround, missing diagnostics, or evidence gaps that would mislead product-observed closure.
severityEnum.observableExample: Gameplay can finish, but console errors, missing frame stats, or absent replay prevent a product-observed pass.

severityEnum.definition: The core flow works and evidence exists, but usability, clarity, presentation, or ergonomics fall below practical quality expectations.
severityEnum.observableExample: HUD wording or visual alignment is confusing but does not block the observed scenario.

ownerMapping.runtime: Runtime/data-plane owner: Rust contracts plus browser runtime evidence, depending on surface.
ownerMapping.studio: Studio/editor owner: presentation/control-plane UX with Rust-owned artifact truth and gated writes.
ownerMapping.core: Core gameplay/data owner: Rust validation and deterministic evidence contracts.
ownerMapping.agentic: Agentic-loop owner: proposal/review/apply/rerun evidence and human gate preservation.
ownerMapping.qa: QA/evaluator owner: failure taxonomy, scenario coverage, verdict quality, and gap escalation.
ownerMapping.export: Export/package owner: local package descriptors, provenance, checksums, and runnable smoke evidence.
ownerMapping.product: Dogfood/product owner: real-game slice quality, human fun/taste verdicts, and release-go/no-go boundaries.

classificationRules: Use exactly one category id from categoryEnum and one severity id from severityEnum per primary finding.
Use blocking when the claimed product flow cannot complete or required evidence is absent for product-observed closure.
Use major when the flow completes only with misleading gaps, diagnostics failures, or substantial workaround.
Use polish only when product-observed completion remains honest and the issue is usability/presentation refinement.
Record gaps as backlog items rather than changing failed product observations to green.
